description: Unexplored lead-braking family: ego follows a passenger car on a normal road when the lead vehicle performs a fixed hard brake; the left escape corridor is blocked by a median/barrier, the right lane is open, and a follower behind ego creates rear-impact risk if ego brakes hard. The lead vehicle is instantiated on a collision course for each TTC/ego-speed point, with fixed lead deceleration of 8 m/s^2.
hypothesis: With a close follower and short actionable TTC, right lane-change/escape should dominate hard braking; as TTC increases or follower gap grows, controlled braking should dominate despite the lead-braking threat.
